warfarin.message: Caution: These foods are high in Vitamin K, which reduces the effectiveness of your blood thinner.

atorvastatin.message: Grapefruit juice can increase the levels of Statin in your blood to dangerous levels.

metformin.message: Risk of lactic acidosis increases when taken with heavy alcohol consumption.

lisinopril.message: This ACE inhibitor can increase potassium levels. Excessive intake of high-potassium foods may cause heart palpitations.

levothyroxine.message: These ingredients can make this thyroid medication less effective. Take medication on an empty stomach.

amlodipine.message: Grapefruit may slightly increase the concentration of the drug in your system.

spironolactone.message: Avoid salt substitutes containing potassium as they can lead to dangerously high potassium levels (hyperkalemia).

ciprofloxacin.message: Calcium-rich dairy products can bind to the antibiotic and prevent its absorption.
